revision.comment: translated from Japanese
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Naka Ward, Nagoya, Japan}} [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|thumb|The ''haiden'' of the Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|thumb|Historic print with an aerial view of the shrine, from the ''[[Owari meisho zue]]'' (1880)]] The '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社 ''Wakamiya Hachimansha'') in [[Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|Suehiro-chō]], [[Sakae, Nagoya|Sakae]] 3-[[chōme]] in the [[Naka-ku, Nagoya|Naka ward]] of [[Nagoya]] is a historic [[Shinto shrine]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/spot/detail/112/|title = 若宮八幡社 | 【公式】名古屋市観光情報「名古屋コンシェルジュ」}} The shrine's record tells that it dates back to the reign of Emperor Monmu (697-707) in [[Taihō era]] (701-704) and was restored during the Engi era (901-23).{{cite web | website=kikuko's web site | url=http://kikuko-nagoya.com/html/wakamiya-hachimansha.html | access-date=2023-03-01|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine - Wakamiya Hachiman-sha}} Shōgun Tokugawa leyasu (1542-1616) relocated the shrine to the present site in 1610. It was the main shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa family]]. The shrine was destroyed in the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], but rebuilt in 1957.{{Cite web|url=https://centrip-japan.com/article/190.html|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine (in Nagoya)|website=Centrip Japan}} The shrine's festival is carried out on May 15 and 16 every year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/en/event/detail/11/|title = Wakamiya Festival}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nipponnews.net/features/traditional-japan-features/wakamiya-hachiman-sha-shrine-festival-in-nagoya/|title=Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine festival in Nagoya|date=17 May 2018}} Its festival float "Fukurokuju-sha" (Fukurokuju is one of seven gods of good luck) is a registered cultural property of the city. ==Overview== [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] {{Shrine |名称 = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|250px]]
Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden
([[2020]] (Reiwa 2) February){{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]] 3-35-30 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|48.5|N|136|54|12.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[Emperor Nintoku]]
[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] |社格 = Former [[Prefectural shrine]], [[Beppyo shrine]] |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |別名 = Wakamiya Hachiman-gū |札所等 = |例祭 = May 16 |神事 = Wakamiya Matsuri (May 15-16) }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (わかみやはちまんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine was formerly ranked as a [[Prefectural shrine]] and is now a [[Beppyo shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of [[Emperor Tenmu]], or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, [[1532]] (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when [[Oda Nobuhide]] attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in [[1539]] (Tenmon 8). During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. In [[1610]] (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Nagoya Castle. During the [[Edo period]], it became the tutelary shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In [[1664]] (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In [[1689]] (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, [[1877]] (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Prefectural shrine]]. During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya Festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == Festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+Festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's Festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer Festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine Festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal Festival |- |16日||Main Festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint Festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer Festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity Festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine Festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine Festival |- |23日||Harvest Festival |} === Main Festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya Festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū Festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno Festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion Festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno Festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya Festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya Festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya City]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Sakae (Nagoya City)]] [[Category:Designated tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Official website] {{commons category-inline|Wakamiya-hachimansha}} {{coord|35.16330|136.90341|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagoya]] [[Category:Sakae, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Dd]] [[Category:Gg]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:若宮八幡社]] {{Japan-hist-stub}}

During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya Festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == Festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+Festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's Festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer Festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine Festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal Festival |- |16日||Main Festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint Festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer Festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity Festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine Festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine Festival |- |23日||Harvest Festival |} === Main Festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya Festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū Festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno Festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion Festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno Festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya Festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya Festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Naka Ward, Nagoya, Japan}} [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|thumb|The ''haiden'' of the Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|thumb|Historic print with an aerial view of the shrine, from the ''[[Owari meisho zue]]'' (1880)]] The '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社 ''Wakamiya Hachimansha'') in [[Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|Suehiro-chō]], [[Sakae, Nagoya|Sakae]] 3-[[chōme]] in the [[Naka-ku, Nagoya|Naka ward]] of [[Nagoya]] is a historic [[Shinto shrine]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/spot/detail/112/|title = 若宮八幡社 | 【公式】名古屋市観光情報「名古屋コンシェルジュ」}} The shrine's record tells that it dates back to the reign of Emperor Monmu (697-707) in [[Taihō era]] (701-704) and was restored during the Engi era (901-23).{{cite web | website=kikuko's web site | url=http://kikuko-nagoya.com/html/wakamiya-hachimansha.html | access-date=2023-03-01|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine - Wakamiya Hachiman-sha}} Shōgun Tokugawa leyasu (1542-1616) relocated the shrine to the present site in 1610. It was the main shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa family]]. The shrine was destroyed in the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], but rebuilt in 1957.{{Cite web|url=https://centrip-japan.com/article/190.html|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine (in Nagoya)|website=Centrip Japan}} The shrine's festival is carried out on May 15 and 16 every year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/en/event/detail/11/|title = Wakamiya Festival}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nipponnews.net/features/traditional-japan-features/wakamiya-hachiman-sha-shrine-festival-in-nagoya/|title=Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine festival in Nagoya|date=17 May 2018}} Its festival float "Fukurokuju-sha" (Fukurokuju is one of seven gods of good luck) is a registered cultural property of the city. ==Overview== [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] {{Shrine |名称 = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|250px]]
Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden
([[2020]] (Reiwa 2) February){{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]] 3-35-30 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|48.5|N|136|54|12.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[Emperor Nintoku]]
[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] |社格 = Former [[Prefectural shrine]], [[Beppyo shrine]] |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |別名 = Wakamiya Hachiman-gū |札所等 = |例祭 = May 16 |神事 = Wakamiya Matsuri (May 15-16) }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (わかみやはちまんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine was formerly ranked as a [[Prefectural shrine]] and is now a [[Beppyo shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of [[Emperor Tenmu]], or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, [[1532]] (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when [[Oda Nobuhide]] attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in [[1539]] (Tenmon 8). During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. In [[1610]] (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Nagoya Castle. During the [[Edo period]], it became the tutelary shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In [[1664]] (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In [[1689]] (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, [[1877]] (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Prefectural shrine]]. During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya Festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == Festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+Festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's Festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer Festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine Festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal Festival |- |16日||Main Festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint Festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer Festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity Festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine Festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine Festival |- |23日||Harvest Festival |} === Main Festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya Festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū Festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno Festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion Festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno Festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya Festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya Festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya City]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Sakae (Nagoya City)]] [[Category:Designated tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == In the current Wakamiya Festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya Festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya City]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Sakae (Nagoya City)]] [[Category:Designated tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Official website] {{commons category-inline|Wakamiya-hachimansha}} {{coord|35.16330|136.90341|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagoya]] [[Category:Sakae, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:若宮八幡社]] {{Japan-hist-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Naka Ward, Nagoya, Japan}} [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|thumb|The ''haiden'' of the Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|thumb|Historic print with an aerial view of the shrine, from the ''[[Owari meisho zue]]'' (1880)]] The '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社 ''Wakamiya Hachimansha'') in [[Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|Suehiro-chō]], [[Sakae, Nagoya|Sakae]] 3-[[chōme]] in the [[Naka-ku, Nagoya|Naka ward]] of [[Nagoya]] is a historic [[Shinto shrine]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/spot/detail/112/|title = 若宮八幡社 | 【公式】名古屋市観光情報「名古屋コンシェルジュ」}} The shrine's record tells that it dates back to the reign of Emperor Monmu (697-707) in [[Taihō era]] (701-704) and was restored during the Engi era (901-23).{{cite web | website=kikuko's web site | url=http://kikuko-nagoya.com/html/wakamiya-hachimansha.html | access-date=2023-03-01|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine - Wakamiya Hachiman-sha}} Shōgun Tokugawa leyasu (1542-1616) relocated the shrine to the present site in 1610. It was the main shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa family]]. The shrine was destroyed in the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], but rebuilt in 1957.{{Cite web|url=https://centrip-japan.com/article/190.html|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine (in Nagoya)|website=Centrip Japan}} The shrine's festival is carried out on May 15 and 16 every year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/en/event/detail/11/|title = Wakamiya Festival}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nipponnews.net/features/traditional-japan-features/wakamiya-hachiman-sha-shrine-festival-in-nagoya/|title=Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine festival in Nagoya|date=17 May 2018}} Its festival float "Fukurokuju-sha" (Fukurokuju is one of seven gods of good luck) is a registered cultural property of the city. ==Overview== [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] {{Shrine |名称 = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|250px]]
Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden
([[2020]] (Reiwa 2) February){{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]] 3-35-30 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|48.5|N|136|54|12.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[Emperor Nintoku]]
[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] |社格 = Former [[Prefectural shrine]], [[Beppyo shrine]] |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |別名 = Wakamiya Hachiman-gū |札所等 = |例祭 = May 16 |神事 = Wakamiya Matsuri (May 15-16) }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (わかみやはちまんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine was formerly ranked as a [[Prefectural shrine]] and is now a [[Beppyo shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of [[Emperor Tenmu]], or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, [[1532]] (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when [[Oda Nobuhide]] attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in [[1539]] (Tenmon 8). During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. In [[1610]] (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Nagoya Castle. During the [[Edo period]], it became the tutelary shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In [[1664]] (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In [[1689]] (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, [[1877]] (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Prefectural shrine]]. During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya Festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == Festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+Festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's Festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer Festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine Festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal Festival |- |16日||Main Festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint Festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer Festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity Festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine Festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine Festival |- |23日||Harvest Festival |} === Main Festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya Festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū Festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno Festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion Festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno Festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya Festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya Festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya City]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Sakae (Nagoya City)]] [[Category:Designated tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Official website] {{commons category-inline|Wakamiya-hachimansha}} {{coord|35.16330|136.90341|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagoya]] [[Category:Sakae, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:若宮八幡社]] {{Japan-hist-stub}}

{{Short description|Shrine in Naka Ward, Nagoya, Japan}} [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|thumb|The ''haiden'' of the Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|thumb|Historic print with an aerial view of the shrine, from the ''[[Owari meisho zue]]'' (1880)]] The '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社 ''Wakamiya Hachimansha'') in [[Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|Suehiro-chō]], [[Sakae, Nagoya|Sakae]] 3-[[chōme]] in the [[Naka-ku, Nagoya|Naka ward]] of [[Nagoya]] is a historic [[Shinto shrine]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/spot/detail/112/|title = 若宮八幡社 | 【公式】名古屋市観光情報「名古屋コンシェルジュ」}} The shrine's record tells that it dates back to the reign of Emperor Monmu (697-707) in [[Taihō era]] (701-704) and was restored during the Engi era (901-23).{{cite web | website=kikuko's web site | url=http://kikuko-nagoya.com/html/wakamiya-hachimansha.html | access-date=2023-03-01|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine - Wakamiya Hachiman-sha}} Shōgun Tokugawa leyasu (1542-1616) relocated the shrine to the present site in 1610. It was the main shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa family]]. The shrine was destroyed in the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], but rebuilt in 1957.{{Cite web|url=https://centrip-japan.com/article/190.html|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine (in Nagoya)|website=Centrip Japan}} The shrine's festival is carried out on May 15 and 16 every year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/en/event/detail/11/|title = Wakamiya Festival}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nipponnews.net/features/traditional-japan-features/wakamiya-hachiman-sha-shrine-festival-in-nagoya/|title=Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine festival in Nagoya|date=17 May 2018}} Its festival float "Fukurokuju-sha" (Fukurokuju is one of seven gods of good luck) is a registered cultural property of the city. ==Overview== [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] {{Shrine |名称 = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|250px]]
Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden
([[2020]] (Reiwa 2) February){{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]] 3-35-30 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|48.5|N|136|54|12.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[Emperor Nintoku]]
[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] |社格 = Former [[Prefectural shrine]], [[Beppyo shrine]] |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |別名 = Wakamiya Hachiman-gū |札所等 = |例祭 = May 16 |神事 = Wakamiya Matsuri (May 15-16) }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (わかみやはちまんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine was formerly ranked as a [[Prefectural shrine]] and is now a [[Beppyo shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of [[Emperor Tenmu]], or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, [[1532]] (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when [[Oda Nobuhide]] attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in [[1539]] (Tenmon 8). During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. In [[1610]] (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Nagoya Castle. During the [[Edo period]], it became the tutelary shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In [[1664]] (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In [[1689]] (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, [[1877]] (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Prefectural shrine]]. During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya Festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == Festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+Festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's Festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer Festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine Festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine Festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal Festival |- |16日||Main Festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint Festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer Festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity Festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine Festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine Festival |- |23日||Harvest Festival |} === Main Festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya Festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū Festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno Festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion Festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno Festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya Festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya Festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya City]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Sakae (Nagoya City)]] [[Category:Designated tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Official website] {{commons category-inline|Wakamiya-hachimansha}} {{coord|35.16330|136.90341|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagoya]] [[Category:Sakae, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:若宮八幡社]] {{Japan-hist-stub}}

It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of [[Emperor Tenmu]], or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, [[1532]] (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when [[Oda Nobuhide]] attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in [[1539]] (Tenmon 8). During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. In [[1610]] (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Nagoya Castle. During the [[Edo period]], it became the tutelary shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In [[1664]] (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In [[1689]] (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, [[1877]] (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Prefectural shrine]]. During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal festival |- |16日||Main festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine festival |- |23日||Harvest festival |} === Main festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya City]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Sakae (Nagoya City)]] [[Category:Designated tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Official website] {{commons category-inline|Wakamiya-hachimansha}} {{coord|35.16330|136.90341|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] {{Short description|Shrine in Naka Ward, Nagoya, Japan}} [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|thumb|The ''haiden'' of the Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|thumb|Historic print with an aerial view of the shrine, from the ''[[Owari meisho zue]]'' (1880)]] The '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社 ''Wakamiya Hachimansha'') in [[Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|Suehiro-chō]], [[Sakae, Nagoya|Sakae]] 3-[[chōme]] in the [[Naka-ku, Nagoya|Naka ward]] of [[Nagoya]] is a historic [[Shinto shrine]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/spot/detail/112/|title = 若宮八幡社 | 【公式】名古屋市観光情報「名古屋コンシェルジュ」}} The shrine's record tells that it dates back to the reign of Emperor Monmu (697-707) in [[Taihō era]] (701-704) and was restored during the Engi era (901-23).{{cite web | website=kikuko's web site | url=http://kikuko-nagoya.com/html/wakamiya-hachimansha.html | access-date=2023-03-01|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine - Wakamiya Hachiman-sha}} Shōgun Tokugawa leyasu (1542-1616) relocated the shrine to the present site in 1610. It was the main shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa family]]. The shrine was destroyed in the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], but rebuilt in 1957.{{Cite web|url=https://centrip-japan.com/article/190.html|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine (in Nagoya)|website=Centrip Japan}} The shrine's festival is carried out on May 15 and 16 every year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/en/event/detail/11/|title = Wakamiya festival}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nipponnews.net/features/traditional-japan-features/wakamiya-hachiman-sha-shrine-festival-in-nagoya/|title=Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine festival in Nagoya|date=17 May 2018}} Its festival float "Fukurokuju-sha" (Fukurokuju is one of seven gods of good luck) is a registered cultural property of the city. ==Overview== [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] {{Shrine |名称 = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |画像 = [[image:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|250px]]
Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden

[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] |社格 = Former [[Prefectural shrine]], [[Beppyo shrine]] |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |別名 = Wakamiya Hachiman-gū |札所等 = |例祭 = May 16 |神事 = Wakamiya Matsuri (May 15-16) }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (わかみやはちまんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine was formerly ranked as a [[Prefectural shrine]] and is now a [[Beppyo shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of [[Emperor Tenmu]], or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, [[1532]] (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when [[Oda Nobuhide]] attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in [[1539]] (Tenmon 8). During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. In [[1610]] (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Nagoya Castle. During the [[Edo period]], it became the tutelary shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In [[1664]] (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In [[1689]] (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, [[1877]] (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Prefectural shrine]]. During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal festival |- |16日||Main festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine festival |- |23日||Harvest festival |} === Main festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] {{Short description|Shrine in Naka Ward, Nagoya, Japan}} [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|thumb|The ''haiden'' of the Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|thumb|Historic print with an aerial view of the shrine, from the ''[[Owari meisho zue]]'' (1880)]] The '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社 ''Wakamiya Hachimansha'') in [[Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|Suehiro-chō]], [[Sakae, Nagoya|Sakae]] 3-[[chōme]] in the [[Naka-ku, Nagoya|Naka ward]] of [[Nagoya]] is a historic [[Shinto shrine]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/spot/detail/112/|title = 若宮八幡社 | 【公式】名古屋市観光情報「名古屋コンシェルジュ」}} The shrine's record tells that it dates back to the reign of Emperor Monmu (697-707) in [[Taihō era]] (701-704) and was restored during the Engi era (901-23).{{cite web | website=kikuko's web site | url=http://kikuko-nagoya.com/html/wakamiya-hachimansha.html | access-date=2023-03-01|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine - Wakamiya Hachiman-sha}} Shōgun Tokugawa leyasu (1542-1616) relocated the shrine to the present site in 1610. It was the main shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa family]]. The shrine was destroyed in the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], but rebuilt in 1957.{{Cite web|url=https://centrip-japan.com/article/190.html|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine (in Nagoya)|website=Centrip Japan}} The shrine's festival is carried out on May 15 and 16 every year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/en/event/detail/11/|title = Wakamiya festival}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nipponnews.net/features/traditional-japan-features/wakamiya-hachiman-sha-shrine-festival-in-nagoya/|title=Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine festival in Nagoya|date=17 May 2018}} Its festival float "Fukurokuju-sha" (Fukurokuju is one of seven gods of good luck) is a registered cultural property of the city. ==Overview== [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] {{Shrine |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[image:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|250px]]
Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden
([[2020]] (Reiwa 2) February){{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |address = [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]] 3-35-30 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|48.5|N|136|54|12.4|E|scale:25000}} |deity = [[Emperor Nintoku]]
[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural shrine]], [[Beppyo shrine]] |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |別名 = Wakamiya Hachiman-gū |札所等 = |例祭 = May 16 |神事 = Wakamiya Matsuri (May 15-16) }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (わかみやはちまんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine was formerly ranked as a [[Prefectural shrine]] and is now a [[Beppyo shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of [[Emperor Tenmu]], or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, [[1532]] (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when [[Oda Nobuhide]] attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in [[1539]] (Tenmon 8). During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. In [[1610]] (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Nagoya Castle. During the [[Edo period]], it became the tutelary shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In [[1664]] (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In [[1689]] (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, [[1877]] (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Prefectural shrine]]. During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal festival |- |16日||Main festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine festival |- |23日||Harvest festival |} === Main festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya City]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Sakae (Nagoya City)]] [[Category:Designated tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Official website] {{commons category-inline|Wakamiya-hachimansha}} {{coord|35.16330|136.90341|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagoya]] [[Category:Sakae, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] {{Short description|Shrine in Naka Ward, Nagoya, Japan}} [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|thumb|The ''haiden'' of the Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|thumb|Historic print with an aerial view of the shrine, from the ''[[Owari meisho zue]]'' (1880)]] The '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社 ''Wakamiya Hachimansha'') in [[Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|Suehiro-chō]], [[Sakae, Nagoya|Sakae]] 3-[[chōme]] in the [[Naka-ku, Nagoya|Naka ward]] of [[Nagoya]] is a historic [[Shinto shrine]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/spot/detail/112/|title = 若宮八幡社 | 【公式】名古屋市観光情報「名古屋コンシェルジュ」}} The shrine's record tells that it dates back to the reign of Emperor Monmu (697-707) in [[Taihō era]] (701-704) and was restored during the Engi era (901-23).{{cite web | website=kikuko's web site | url=http://kikuko-nagoya.com/html/wakamiya-hachimansha.html | access-date=2023-03-01|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine - Wakamiya Hachiman-sha}} Shōgun Tokugawa leyasu (1542-1616) relocated the shrine to the present site in 1610. It was the main shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa family]]. The shrine was destroyed in the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], but rebuilt in 1957.{{Cite web|url=https://centrip-japan.com/article/190.html|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine (in Nagoya)|website=Centrip Japan}} The shrine's festival is carried out on May 15 and 16 every year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/en/event/detail/11/|title = Wakamiya festival}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nipponnews.net/features/traditional-japan-features/wakamiya-hachiman-sha-shrine-festival-in-nagoya/|title=Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine festival in Nagoya|date=17 May 2018}} Its festival float "Fukurokuju-sha" (Fukurokuju is one of seven gods of good luck) is a registered cultural property of the city. ==Overview== [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] {{Shrine |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[image:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|250px]]
Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden
([[2020]] (Reiwa 2) February){{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |address = [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]] 3-35-30 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|48.5|N|136|54|12.4|E|scale:25000}} |deity = [[Emperor Nintoku]]
[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] |rank = Former [[Prefectural shrine]], [[Beppyo shrine]] |established = Unknown |main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative name = Wakamiya Hachiman-gū |札所等 = |例祭 = May 16 |festival = Wakamiya Matsuri (May 15-16) }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (わかみやはちまんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Nagoya City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine was formerly ranked as a [[Prefectural shrine]] and is now a [[Beppyo shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of [[Emperor Tenmu]], or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, [[1532]] (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when [[Oda Nobuhide]] attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in [[1539]] (Tenmon 8). During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. In [[1610]] (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Nagoya Castle. During the [[Edo period]], it became the tutelary shrine of the [[Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In [[1664]] (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In [[1689]] (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, [[1877]] (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Prefectural shrine]]. During the [[Pacific War]], the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in [[1957]] (Shōwa 32). On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal festival |- |16日||Main festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine festival |- |23日||Harvest festival |} === Main festival === During the [[Edo period]], the main Wakamiya festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from [[Ōsu Kannon Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Tsurumai Line]], or from [[Yabachō Station]] on the [[Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Subway]] [[Meijō Line]]. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", [[1844]] == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by [[Akita Shoten]]) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, Nagoya Naka Ward}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya City]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Sakae (Nagoya City)]] [[Category:Designated tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagoya City Naka Ward]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * During the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]], the shrine received 200 koku from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. On July 1, [[1971]] (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - [[Emperor Nintoku]], [[Emperor Ōjin]], [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri [[Inari Shrine]] * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right;
