revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Wakamiya-hachimansha]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[ff:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[co:commons:Category:Wakamiya-hachimansha]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[ff:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[ff:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] {{Short description|Shrine in Naka Ward, Nagoya, Japan}} [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|thumb|The ''haiden'' of the Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|thumb|Historic print with an aerial view of the shrine, from the ''{{ill|Owari meisho zue|en|Owari meisho zue|WD=Q11465290|lt=Owari meisho zue|1=Owari meisho zue}}'' (1880)]] The '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社 ''Wakamiya Hachimansha'') in {{ill|Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|en|Suehiro-chō, Nagoya|WD=Q20045074|lt=Suehiro-chō|1=Suehiro-chō, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Sakae, Nagoya|en|Sakae, Nagoya|WD=Q1138623|lt=Sakae|1=Sakae, Nagoya}} 3-[[:en:Chōme|Chōme]] in the {{ill|Naka-ku, Nagoya|en|Naka-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1207921|lt=Naka ward|1=Naka-ku, Nagoya}} of {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} is a historic {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/spot/detail/112/|title = 若宮八幡社 | 【公式】名古屋市観光情報「名古屋コンシェルジュ」}} The shrine's record tells that it dates back to the reign of Emperor Monmu (697-707) in [[:en:Taihō era|Taihō era]] (701-704) and was restored during the Engi era (901-23).{{cite web | website=kikuko's web site | url=http://kikuko-nagoya.com/html/wakamiya-hachimansha.html | access-date=2023-03-01|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine - Wakamiya Hachiman-sha}} Shōgun Tokugawa leyasu (1542-1616) relocated the shrine to the present site in 1610. It was the main shrine of the {{ill|Owari Tokugawa family|en|Owari Tokugawa family|WD=Q841740|lt=Owari Tokugawa family|1=Owari Tokugawa family}}. The shrine was destroyed in the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], but rebuilt in 1957.{{Cite web|url=https://centrip-japan.com/article/190.html|title=Wakamiya Hachimansha Shrine (in Nagoya)|website=Centrip Japan}} The shrine's festival is carried out on May 15 and 16 every year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nagoya-info.jp/en/event/detail/11/|title = Wakamiya festival}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nipponnews.net/features/traditional-japan-features/wakamiya-hachiman-sha-shrine-festival-in-nagoya/|title=Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine festival in Nagoya|date=17 May 2018}} Its festival float "Fukurokuju-sha" (Fukurokuju is one of seven gods of good luck) is a registered cultural property of the city. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |image =
Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden
({{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}} (Reiwa 2) February){{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, [[:en:Nagoya City|Nagoya City]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]] 3-35-30 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|48.5|N|136|54|12.4|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
[[Takenouchi no Sukune]] |Rank = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]], [[Beppyo Shrine]] |established = Unknown |main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] |alternative name = Wakamiya Hachiman-gū |札所等 = |festival = Wakamiya Matsuri (May 15-16) }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (わかみやはちまんしゃ) is a shrine located in [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya City|Nagoya City]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. == Overview == The shrine was formerly Ranked as a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] and is now a [[Beppyo Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is regarded as the general guardian shrine of Nagoya. The name of "Wakamiya Ōdōri" (100m Road) running east-west through Nagoya city is derived from Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine. It is also known as "Wakamiya Hachiman-gū". == History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the era of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, or during the [[Daihō era]] (701-704), in Nakono-shō Ichiiba (present-day Sannomaru of [[Nagoya Castle]]), and was rebuilt during the [[:en:Engi era|Engi era]] (901-923). It was once adjacent to the [[Tenno Shrine]] (present-day [[Nagono Shrine]]). On March 11, {{ill|1532|en|1532|WD=Q6436|lt=1532|1=1532}} (Tenmon 1), the shrine was burned down in the battle when {{ill|Oda Nobuhide|en|Oda Nobuhide|WD=Q1153962|lt=Oda Nobuhide|1=Oda Nobuhide}} attacked [[Nagono Castle]], but was rebuilt by Nobuhide in {{ill|1539|en|1539|WD=Q6479|lt=1539|1=1539}} (Tenmon 8). During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, the shrine received 200 koku from {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. In {{ill|1610|en|1610|WD=Q6765|lt=1610|1=1610}} (Keichō 15), the shrine was relocated to its present location when {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Nagoya Castle. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it became the tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Owari Tokugawa clan|Owari Tokugawa clan]] and was revered as the general guardian of Nagoya. In {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664|1=1664}} (Kanbun 4), the second lord of the Owari Domain, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|en|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|WD=Q843245|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|1=Tokugawa Mitsutomo}}, had the shrine rebuilt. At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In {{ill|1689|en|1689|WD=Q7674|lt=1689|1=1689}} (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]]. During the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}} (Shōwa 32). On July 1, {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[:en:Tenjin Shrine|Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal festival |- |16日||Main festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine festival |- |23日||Harvest festival |} === Main festival === During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the main Wakamiya festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya festival") == Access == * About a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ōsu Kannon Station|en|Ōsu Kannon Station|WD=Q862936|lt=Ōsu Kannon Station|1=Ōsu Kannon Station}} on the {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|en|Nagoya Municipal Subway|WD=Q1144874|lt=Nagoya Subway|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}} {{ill|Tsurumai Line|en|Tsurumai Line|WD=Q1043806|lt=Tsurumai Line|1=Tsurumai Line}}, or from {{ill|Yabachō Station|en|Yabachō Station|WD=Q863884|lt=Yabachō Station|1=Yabachō Station}} on the {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|en|Nagoya Municipal Subway|WD=Q1144874|lt=Nagoya Subway|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}} {{ill|Meijō Line|en|Meijō Line|WD=Q906775|lt=Meijō Line|1=Meijō Line}}. == References == * "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine", {{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844|1=1844}} == Related Articles == * [[Shibariya Komachi]]: A fantasy manga themed around [[Onmyōdō]], featuring the "Goshiki no Ito", an amulet based on the "Five-Colored Thread Amulet" from this shrinePrincess Comics "Shibariya Komachi" Vol. 4 (published by {{ill|Akita Shoten|en|Akita Shoten|WD=Q419352|lt=Akita Shoten|1=Akita Shoten}}) comment in the front page. * [[List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == Notes == == External Links == {{osm box|w|110185096}} * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Nagoya General Guardian Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] (official website) * [http://wakamiya8diary.blog12.fc2.com/ Wakamiya Diary] (official blog) * {{commonscat-inline}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Aichi-prefecture-stub}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.wakamiya.or.jp/ Official website] {{commons Category-inline|Wakamiya-hachimansha}} {{coord|35.16330|136.90341|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)|version=104615049|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Wakamiya Hachiman-sha Shrine Haiden, Sakae Naka Ward Nagoya 2020.jpg|250px]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Haiden
({{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}} (Reiwa 2) February){{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, [[:en:Nagoya City|Nagoya City]], [[Naka Ward (Nagoya City)|Naka Ward]], [[Sakae (Nagoya City)|Sakae]] 3-35-30 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|48.5|N|136|54|12.4|E|scale:25000}} |deity = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}

At this time, all the shrine monks were abolished. In {{ill|1689|en|1689|WD=Q7674|lt=1689|1=1689}} (Genroku 2), Mitsutomo also granted 100 koku to the shrine. On March 14, {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (Meiji 10), the shrine was promoted to a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]]. During the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine buildings were destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), but were rebuilt in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}} (Shōwa 32). On July 1, {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} (Shōwa 46), the shrine was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == Deities == * Main deities - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] == Grounds == * Main hall * Worship hall * Wakamiya [[Ebisu Shrine]] * Combined shrine[http://utusemibiyori.com/blog-entry-1427.html] - [[Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]], [[Hie Shrine]], [[Karasu Shrine]], [[:en:Tenjin Shrine|Tenjin Shrine]], [[Akiba Shrine]] * Kamigoromo Shrine (御衣神社, Mikoromo-san) - enshrined from [[Tatsuta Village]] (present-day [[Aisai City]])[http://www.ohsu.co.jp/ohsu/needle.html Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri)]. * San-no-miya [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * Renri {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} * Renri Inari Shrine Inner Shrine * [[Fukurokuju]]-kuruma Storage * Wakamiya [[Ryūjin]] Shrine * Shrine office * Wakamiya no Yashiro Reception Hall File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮八幡宮.jpg|Former Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine") == Cultural Properties == === Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * Wakamiya festival's Dashi float event and Fukurokuju Float (Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Fukurokuju Float Preservation Association) == festivals == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; font-size:small; margin-top: 0px;" |+festival Calendar |1月||1日||New Year's festival (Hōsan-kai Prayer festival) |- |rowspan=3|2月||8日||Kamigoromo Shrine festival (Hari Kuyō Matsuri) |- |17日||Prayers for a Good Harvest |- |旧初午の日||Renri Inari Shrine festival (Kyu-shohun Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|5月||15日||Rehearsal festival |- |16日||Main festival and Divine Procession (Wakamiya Matsuri) |- |7月||25日||Joint festival of Auxiliary Shrines (Summer festival) |- |9月||15日||Longevity festival |- |10月||20日||Ebisu Shrine festival (Ebisu Matsuri) |- |rowspan=2|11月||1日||Ryūjin Shrine festival |- |23日||Harvest festival |} === Main festival === During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the main Wakamiya festival was one of the three major festivals of Nagoya, along with the Tōshōgū festival at [[Nagoya Tōshōgū]] and the Tenno festival at [[Nagono Shrine]], collectively referred to as the [[Gion festival]], as it was held on the same day as the Tenno festival (currently on separate days). Seven floats and a mikoshi processed to and from Nagono Shrine, parading through Honmachi Street, the main street of Nagoya Castle Town. In the current Wakamiya festival, one remaining float ([[Fukurokuju]] Float) and a mikoshi process to and from Nagono Shrine. The Fukurokuju Float is designated as a [[Nagoya City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property]]{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-10-19|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000008502.html|title=City Designated Cultural Properties|publisher=Nagoya City|accessdate=2013-05-26}}. File:Wakamiya1.jpg|Fukurokuju Float in the shrine precincts File:尾張名所図会. 前編 巻１ 愛智郡 若宮祭.jpg|Wakamiya festival (from "Meisho Zue: Owari, First Volume, Aichi District", "Wakamiya festival") == Access ==
